title: Listening - Part 1
description: You are going to read a magazine article about an intellectual process known as critical thinking. For questions 31 – 36, choose the answer (A, B, C or D) which fits best according to the text. Mark your answers on the separate answer sheet.
instructions: You will hear three different extracts. For questions 1–6, choose the answer (A, B or C) which fits best according to what you hear. There are two questions for each extract.

extracts.context: You hear two friends discussing a new city tour focused on street art.
extracts.audio_script: Man: That 'Graffiti-Walk' was much more interesting than the usual historic bus tours. || Woman: [1]I found the guide's tendency to wander off the main topic a bit frustrating[/1], though. I wanted to hear about the artists, not his personal life. || Man: I see what you mean. He did go on a few tangents. || Woman: [2]What I really appreciated, though, was the way the tour took us through parts of the city I’d never visited[/2]. Some of those backstreets were fascinating. || Man: I agree. It made me see my own city in a completely different light. || Woman: Exactly. It’s a great concept for a tour, I just wish it was a bit more focused on the art itself.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: That 'Graffiti-Walk' was much more interesting than the usual historic bus tours.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: I found the guide's tendency to wander off the main topic a bit frustrating, though. I wanted to hear about the artists, not his personal life.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I see what you mean. He did go on a few tangents.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: What I really appreciated, though, was the way the tour took us through parts of the city I’d never visited. Some of those backstreets were fascinating.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I agree. It made me see my own city in a completely different light.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Exactly. It’s a great concept for a tour, I just wish it was a bit more focused on the art itself.

questions.question: The woman is critical of the tour guide because

options.A: he lacked knowledge about street art.
options.B: he included too much irrelevant information.
options.C: he walked at an uncomfortably fast pace.

questions.explanation: She found his 'tendency to wander off the main topic' and personal stories frustrating [1].

questions.question: Both speakers liked the way the tour
questions.explanation: They both praise the way it took them through 'parts of the city' they'd 'never visited' [2].

extracts.context: You hear two colleagues talking about a new employee wellness program.
extracts.audio_script: Man: So, have you signed up for the office yoga sessions yet? I’m finding them quite relaxing. || Woman: [3]I suspect the HR department is using this as a cheap alternative to actually addressing our workload issues[/3]. || Man: That's a bit cynical! It’s still a nice perk to have. || Woman: [4]I’ve noticed that the sessions are scheduled during our lunch hour[/4], which means I’m basically working through my break to make up for the time. || Man: I agree. It’s a bit of a contradiction if a wellness program makes you more stressed. || Woman: Exactly. If they really cared about our well-being, they’d hire more staff instead of giving us free mats.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: So, have you signed up for the office yoga sessions yet? I’m finding them quite relaxing.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: I suspect the HR department is used this as a cheap alternative to actually addressing our workload issues.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: That's a bit cynical! It’s still a nice perk to have.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: I’ve noticed that the sessions are scheduled during our lunch hour, which means I’m basically working through my break to make up for the time.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I agree. It’s a bit of a contradiction if a wellness program makes you more stressed.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Exactly. If they really cared about our well-being, they’d hire more staff instead of giving us free mats.

questions.question: The woman believes the wellness program was designed to

options.A: improve the physical health of the staff.
options.C: increase the speed of daily tasks.

questions.explanation: She thinks it's a 'cheap alternative' to addressing 'workload issues' [3].

questions.question: What paradox do both speakers identify regarding the program?

options.A: It is too expensive for the employees to join.
options.B: It contributes to the stress it aims to reduce.
options.C: It is unpopular despite the high-quality facilities.

questions.explanation: They agree it's a 'contradiction' that a wellness program makes people more stressed due to scheduling [4].

extracts.context: You hear two people discussing a recently opened ethical fashion store.
extracts.audio_script: Man: I went to 'Conscious-Closet' yesterday. The quality of the organic cotton shirts is fantastic. || Woman: [5]I found the store's layout to be a bit confusing[/5], to be honest. It wasn't clear what was for men or women. || Man: I see your point. It’s a bit of a 'gender-neutral' free-for-all. || Woman: [6]I did appreciate the transparency regarding their supply chain, though[/6]. Being able to see exactly where each garment was made is great. || Man: I agree. It makes you feel much more comfortable about your purchase. || Woman: Definitely. It’s a step in the right direction for the industry, I just wish they’d organise the racks a bit better.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I went to 'Conscious-Closet' yesterday. The quality of the organic cotton shirts is fantastic.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: I found the store's layout to be a bit confusing, to be honest. It wasn't clear what was for men or women.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I see your point. It’s a bit of a 'gender-neutral' free-for-all.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: I did appreciate the transparency regarding their supply chain, though. Being able to see exactly where each garment was made is great.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I agree. It makes you feel much more comfortable about your purchase.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Definitely. It’s a step in the right direction for the industry, I just wish they’d organise the racks a bit better.

questions.question: What did the woman find annoying about her visit to the store?

options.A: the high cost of the clothing items
options.B: the poor categorization of the products
options.C: the limited variety of sizes available

questions.explanation: She found the layout 'confusing' as it wasn't clear which items were for whom [5].

questions.question: Both speakers were impressed by the store's
questions.explanation: They both praise the 'transparency regarding their supply chain' [6].
